steps.title: Find the vote margin in the poll sample
steps.content: From the table, Angel Cruz received 483 votes and Terry Smith received 320 votes in the poll of 803 total voters. The margin between them is $483 - 320 = 163$ votes.

steps.title: Express margin as a proportion of sample size
steps.content: The margin of 163 votes out of 803 polled represents the expected proportion of the victory margin. If this same proportion holds for the full election, the margin would be: $$\frac{163}{803} \times 6,424$$

steps.title: Simplify using the scaling relationship
steps.content:
Check whether 6,424 and 803 have a relationship: $6,424 \div 803 = 8$, so $6,424 = 803 \times 8$.

Substituting: $$\frac{163}{803} \times (803 \times 8) = 163 \times 8 = 1,304$$
Angel Cruz would be expected to win by 1,304 votes. That matches choice B — B is correct.
